Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient using the MediAI Assist platform.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the patient record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the patient record was last updated.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a doctor on the MediAI Assist platform.

verified.description: Indicates if the doctor's profile has been verified.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the doctor record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the doctor record was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment booked between a patient and a doctor.

appointmentDateTime.description: Date and time of the appointment.

appointmentType.description: Type of appointment (e.g., chat, audio, video).

status.description: Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, cancelled).

notes.description: Additional notes or information about the appointment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the appointment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the appointment record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores patient profiles. Only the authenticated user (patient) with the matching `patientId` can access their own document.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

definition.description: Stores doctor profiles. Only the admin (via a dedicated admin role check) can manage doctor profiles. Patients can read doctor profiles to find suitable healthcare providers.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctor.

definition.description: Stores appointment records. Patients and Doctors have read access to the records with their IDs. Includes `patientId` and `doctorId` within the document to enforce authorization independence.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support MediAI Assist's core features, focusing on doctor-patient interactions, appointment booking, and administrative oversight. To ensure security, scalability, and debuggability, the structure adheres to the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   For appointments, the `patientId` and `doctorId` are stored directly within the appointment document. This allows security rules to validate that only the patient or the doctor associated with the appointment can access it, without needing to perform `get()` operations to check parent documents. This is crucial for maintaining atomic operations and avoiding rule complexity.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Patient and doctor data are stored in separate collections (`/patients` and `/doctors`), reflecting their distinct security requirements.  This segregation simplifies security rules and avoids the need for complex conditional logic within a single collection.

**Access Modeling:**

*   **Private Data (Patients & Doctors):** Patient and Doctor data are stored in top-level collections `patients` and `doctors` respectively.
*   **User-Owned Data (Appointments):** Appointments are created as a top level collection to allow for querying across both patients and doctors, and both `patientId` and `doctorId` are denormalized on the record for simple rule authorization.
